channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Snowflake rockets 36% on earnings beat and plan to spend $6 billion on Amazon cloud
item.description: Snowflake is going deeper with Amazon's Web Services, and plans to use its Arm-based Graviton chips.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed as Wall Street awaits key April inflation reading: Live updates
item.description: April's reading of the personal consumption expenditure price index, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, is due out at 8:30 a.m. on Thursday.

item.title: Meta to start testing AI subscription services, with cheapest plan at $7.99 a month
item.description: Meta confirmed Wednesday that it will begin testing two subscription plans for its AI offerings.

item.title: Zscaler tanks 31% for worst day ever on 'prudent' guidance, sales shakeup
item.description: Zscaler has shed half its value over the last year.

item.title: This bank just lifted its CD yield. Here's where you can nab 4%
item.description: The getting is still good for savers who want to generate solid interest income on their idle cash.

item.title: Amazon's top health exec is stepping down, will be replaced by Amwell co-founder
item.description: The company is tapping Dr. Roy Schoenberg, a cofounder of telemedicine provider Amwell, to replace Lindsay.

item.title: Goldman raises its S&P 500 year-end forecast. It's for one simple reason
item.description: The bank raised its year-end S&P 500 target to 8,000 from 7,600 because of expectations for booming earnings growth.

item.title: Prediction markets regulation proposal by CFTC eyed by White House
item.description: The CFTC has argued it has exclusive authority to regulate the growing prediction market sector rather than state-by-state regulation.

item.title: Traders are skeptical of Iran timeline for Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: Despite reports Iran could return the passageway to normal traffic flows within a month of a peace deal, Kalshi traders thought that was unlikely.

item.title: Oil prices fall more than 5% after Rubio says U.S. will give Iran talks 'every chance to succeed'
item.description: U.S. crude oil prices trim losses as traders assess whether U.S. is nearing a deal with Iran that would restore traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Low-income households face ‘remarkable’ rise in food insecurity in K-shaped economy: New York Fed
item.description: The rising threat of food insecurity is partly why Americans feel worse off than ever before, according to research by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

item.title: Mortgage refinance demand drops 18% as rates hit highest level since August
item.description: Mortgage rates rose to the highest level in nine months, hitting refinance demand hardest. Homebuyers also pulled back but were still more active than last year.

item.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff outlines his plan to turn around his struggling stock
item.description: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said the company’s strategy during its stock slump is to continue delivering strong products for customers and buy back shares.

item.title: Republicans stare down inflation abyss with midterms fast approaching
item.description: Republicans are struggling to find a clear message to battle high prices as President Trump pushes for funding for a White House ballroom.

item.title: Bank of America says prepare for a 'summer correction’ after rally to record highs
item.description: The bank said several technical indicators suggest the advance is becoming stretched, prompting a shift away from chasing gains and toward protecting them.

item.title: Micron is breaking the mold of the trillion-dollar company by multiple measures
item.description: It's not just the memory chip maker's price-to-earnings ratio that stands out among its peers.

item.title: Analysis: What Stephen Miller gets wrong about debt, deficits and immigration
item.description: The White House immigration hawk and deputy chief of staff is pointing the finger in the wrong direction when assigning blame for the federal budget deficit.

item.title: Trump opposes Russia or China taking Iran's highly enriched uranium
item.description: Trump also said the Strait of Hormuz, the vital oil-shipping route, is "going to be open to everybody."

item.title: After Ferrari Luce backlash, Lamborghini CEO says canceling its own EV was the right choice
item.description: Lamborghini CEO said the automaker's decision to kill its EV to focus on plug-in hybrid electric vehicles was "the right way to go" for his company.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase could spend $20 billion on acquisition: 'We are on the lookout'
item.description: A $20 billion deal would be among the largest in JPMorgan's history and could invite regulatory scrutiny given its position as the largest U.S. bank by assets.

item.title: Here’s our monthly update on all 33 portfolio stocks, including 4 to buy right now
item.description: AI stocks aren't the only place to put money to work, Jim Cramer said at the Investing Club's May Monthly Meeting.

item.title: Your credit card could help cut the cost of your summer vacation
item.description: CNBC Select goes over some of the most overlooked credit card benefits that could help you save money on your summer travels.

item.title: Bears load up bets against small-cap stocks ahead of economic data releases
item.description: Despite a 40% rally the past year – compared to 27% in the S&P 500 and 39% in the Nasdaq-100 – options traders look the most bearish on the Russell 2000.

item.title: Alabama asks Supreme Court to allow congressional map that dilutes Black vote
item.description: A three-judge panel in Birmingham, Alabama, found that the state's proposed map of House districts was 'intentionally discriminatory."

item.title: Boeing CEO says company met requirements to increase 737 Max production to 47 jets per month
item.description: Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said the company has met FAA requirements to ramp up its production of 737 Max aircraft to 47 per month.

item.title: Abercrombie shares jump 12% on earnings beat even as Iran conflict hits sales
item.description: Abercrombie & Fitch saw sales in Europe, Middle East and Africa decline by 10%, thanks in large part to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Russia just passed a law allowing its central bank to down drones
item.description: Russia's State Duma has passed a law allowing certain financial institutions to operate anti-drone defense systems after attacks on its territory.

item.title: UK spy chief: Time is running out for the West to confront threats from Russia and China
item.description: Britain and its allies face a “moment of consequence,” according to the head of its intelligence agency.

item.title: Amazon starts selling its AI shopping technology to other retailers
item.description: Amazon said it's already signed up Kate Spade as a customer for its AI shopping technology.

item.title: Healthy Returns: Eli Lilly makes a new push into vaccines — here’s what to know
item.description: The drugmaker announced it will buy three privately held vaccine makers in deals worth almost $4 billion combined.
